title: Research Index
blurb: swift-linter package research and verification records.

documents.topic: Surveys 6 integration shapes; RECOMMENDS Shape F (precomputed conformance oracle), DEFERS live LSP/SourceKit. v1.1.0 adds Q6 (design-space exploration), Q7 (domain framing), full cross-ecosystem literature study, long-term framing. v1.2.0 promotes RECOMMENDATION → DECISION after spike: 136 stdlib refinement pairs extracted; rule consumes auto-generated table. v1.3.0 (2026-05-13) adds Phase 2.5 user-domain scan empirical findings: 28 institute symbol-graph files yield 2 cross-module refinement pairs (Comparison→Equation, Hash→Equation) — far fewer than stdlib's 136 — and BOTH have leaf-name collision because institute uses `Namespace.\`Protocol\`` convention. Rule's leaf-name matching would need to become path-aware to consume institute pairs; user-domain table NOT shipped to swift-linter-rules in this cycle (separate rule-design decision queued).

Thread E Phase 2 — per-rename catalog covering 24 public-DSL compound-identifier + compound-type-name findings across swift-linter (15+3) and swift-linter-primitives (6), plus 1 carry-forward row from swift-primitives-linter-rules. Each row evaluates Shape A (rename only) vs Shape B (rename + typed-primitive adoption) through the maximal-ecosystem-reuse lens, with recommendation + cascade scope + principal-disposition checkbox. Phase 3 execution is gated on principal sign-off of each row.
documents.statusDetail: Principal dispositioned 2026-05-13. All rows have final shapes; the larger.smaller naming principle applies throughout (rules.disabled, rules.effective, severity.default). Three scope ambiguities resolved: 33 internal swift-linter findings get proper renames; brandOwner refactored to primitives.excluding(rules:) with cascade to 3 consumer packages; Row 24 deferred (target Lint.File.Single in swift-manifests). Phase 3 execution proceeds linter-primitives-first.

documents.topic: Cross-package design analysis of three options for canonical-tier rule activation: (A) single-file Lint.swift + default rule-pack umbrella import; (B) Lint/ nested SwiftPM package as the canonical form with single-file as a sugar variant; (C) Lint.Manifest.defaults() factory baked into the linter binary. Recommends B for production use with A as a future Phase-5 sugar form; rejects C on binary-coupling grounds. Tier 2 design analysis grounded in the Phase A PoC and the post-Phase-B.1 decoupling.
documents.statusDetail: Recommends Option B (Lint/ canonical) with Option A deferred to Phase 5. Phase 5 mechanism design (single-file dep-discovery) is open; two candidates surfaced (bundled rule-pack registry vs swift-manifest dep injection) require prototyping before the recommendation becomes a decision.

documents.topic: Phase A proof-of-concept verification of the Lint/ nested-package mechanism. Validates end-to-end that a consumer's Lint/ SwiftPM package activates rule packs via the linter CLI dispatch and that the R5 27-hit invariant holds.

Phase B.1 verification record — swift-linter decoupled from swift-linter-rules. The engine ships zero built-in rules; rule registration is the consumer's responsibility via a Lint/ nested package or (future) single-file umbrella import.
Phase B.4 verification record — wave-1 rules migrated to the post-architecture shape. Confirms each rule's predicate continues to fire post-decouple under the new activation mechanism.
Phase 2.5b cohort gate — Sanitize / TempPath ecosystem promotion verification record covering the cohort's pre-merge invariants.
Architecture cohort Day 1 progress note. Records the in-flight state of Phase A + B.1 + B.4 work and downstream impact on swift-linter consumers.
Code-surface cleanup verification record for the 2026-05-07 cohort. Confirms convention compliance across the touched modules post-cleanup.

Post-push #8 verification gate — R5 27-hit invariant verified via the chain-resolved code path with all parent URLs resolving post-push. Sibling to the prepush-fallback-gate doc which exercised the consumer-only fallback path.

Pre-push #8 verification gate — R5 27-hit invariant verified via the v2 consumer-only fallback path (parent URLs return 404 pre-push; WARN-signal-bearing fallback fires). Sibling to the postpush-chain-gate doc.
